# binfmt test fixtures

`tiny_mz.exe` (64 bytes) and `tiny_ne.exe` (72 bytes) are checked-in binary
files used by `tests/test_binfmt.c` to exercise the MZ/NE/PE classifier. They
are not runnable programs — each carries only the header bytes the classifier
inspects. `tiny_mz.exe` is an `MZ` DOS header with `e_lfanew` (offset `0x3C`)
set to `0`, which the classifier reads as MZ-with-invalid-`e_lfanew` →
`BIN_MZ`; `tiny_ne.exe` is an `MZ` header with `e_lfanew = 0x40` pointing at an
`NE` signature at offset `0x40` → `BIN_NE16`. Both were generated by the POSIX
shell script `make_fixtures` in this directory (`./make_fixtures`), which emits
the exact bytes with `printf`; re-run it to regenerate them.
